hypothesis: If an EGNN-based diffusion model with VE/VP noise schedules, ghost-atom density control, and an energy-based denoiser with HMC refinement (AMDEN) is trained on amorphous-material datasets and optionally conditioned on Young’s modulus, then it will generate density-correct amorphous structures whose RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and energy distributions match MD references for the specified thermal histories and can be steered to a target Young’s modulus, because physics-informed equivariant denoising captures the configurational and property manifolds of amorphous solids.
